title: Moroccan Chicken with Chickpeas & Quinoa
description: Braised chicken thighs in warm spices with chickpeas, tomatoes, and wilted spinach, served over fluffy quinoa. High protein, high fiber, deeply flavorful.

ingredients.name: bone-in chicken thighs, skin removed before cooking

instructions.text: Pat chicken thighs dry. Mix cumin, cinnamon, turmeric, smoked paprika, pepper, and salt in a small bowl. Rub the spice blend all over the chicken. Let sit at room temp while you prep the aromatics.

Heat olive oil in a large heavy pot or Dutch oven over medium-high. Sear chicken thighs flat-side down for 4–5 minutes until deep golden brown. Flip and sear 3 minutes on the other side. Remove to a plate — they won't be cooked through yet.
Reduce heat to medium. Add onion to the same pot, scraping up the fond. Cook 4–5 minutes until softened. Add garlic and ginger, stir 1 minute until fragrant.
Add diced tomatoes, chicken broth, and chickpeas. Stir to combine. Nestle chicken thighs back in, partially submerged. Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and braise 30 minutes.

While chicken braises, combine quinoa and water in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce to low, cover and simmer 15 minutes. Remove from heat and let steam covered 5 more minutes. Fluff with a fork.
Uncover the pot. Stir in baby spinach and let it wilt into the sauce, about 2 minutes. Add lemon juice and zest. Taste and adjust salt. If the sauce is too thin, simmer uncovered 3–4 minutes.
Spoon quinoa into bowls. Place chicken thighs over the quinoa and ladle chickpea-tomato sauce generously over everything. Top with parsley and optional walnuts for crunch.

notes:
The braise improves overnight -- excellent as leftovers. Doubles easily to 4 servings using 8 thighs with everything else doubled.

Walnuts add a nice textural contrast and a small omega-3 boost as a garnish.

Chickpeas and spinach add meaningful fiber -- this is one of the more nutritionally complete dinners in the rotation.
